reasoning:
The resolution criteria checks if Richard Rapport's Elo rating on the Wikipedia FIDE rankings page is at least 1% higher than his baseline on Jan 18, 2026. According to the page's January 2026 data, his rating is 2741. A 1% increase requires reaching an Elo of at least 2768.41 (i.e., 2769). 

For the first resolution date (Jan 25, 2026), the Wikipedia page will almost certainly continue to reflect the January FIDE list, making any rating change impossible. Probability is at the minimal bound.

For the second resolution date (Feb 17, 2026), the Wikipedia page will reflect the February FIDE list. To reach 2769, Rapport would need an enormous gain of 28 points within a single month. For a top-20 player with a K-factor of 10, this would require an absolutely historic performance (e.g., scoring +6 to +8 wins in a classical super-tournament with no losses). Given that Rapport is not participating in the prominent Tata Steel Masters taking place in January 2026, and there are no records of him playing an ongoing classical tournament where he is massively over-performing, the chances of such a meteoric leap in a single month are effectively zero. I assign the lowest permitted probabilities for both dates, strictly adhering to the mandated lower bounds, with a slight increase over time.
